chunk_summary: This section discusses dietary fat's role in childhood nutrition, emphasizing appropriate amounts, quality, and DHA/ARA counseling, with specific guidelines for Indian practices and preterm infants.
learning_objectives: Understand the recommended dietary fat intake percentages for children across different age groups.
Learn the importance of fat quality, including avoiding trans-fat and using diverse oils.
Recognize DHA and ARA requirements for term and preterm infants.
Apply culturally relevant dietary counseling for Indian families.
key_facts: Dietary fat should provide 30–40% of energy for toddlers and 25–35% for older children and adolescents.
Saturated fat intake should be kept below 10% of total energy after 2 years of age.
Industrial trans-fat should be eliminated; FSSAI limits support this recommendation.
Preterm infants require DHA at 30–65 mg/kg/day with an ARA:DHA ratio of 0.5–2.
Visible fat guidance is crucial in Indian practice to prevent inadequate fat intake.
reference_quote: In India, packaged foods should be checked for trans-fat declarations; the FSSAI limit on industrial trans-fat supports this counseling.
document_key_topics: Dietary fat intake recommendations for children
DHA and ARA requirements for term and preterm infants
Culturally relevant dietary counseling in India
